risk.desc: Potential thermal performance issues due to insufficient cooling channel design.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the cooling channel or add additional channels to improve heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of joint integrity failure if the torque applied during assembly exceeds the specified limit.
risk.mit: Ensure that the assembly process adheres strictly to the torque specifications provided.

risk.desc: Insufficient evidence of fastener locking or preload may compromise structural integrity over time.
risk.mit: Add visible locking evidence such as washers or locking inserts and specify preload intent.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment between the finger cartridge and thimble socket due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and conduct thorough quality checks post-manufacture.

risk.desc: Potential wear and tear on the actuator connector due to repeated insertion and removal.
risk.mit: Use durable materials for the actuator connector and ensure it is designed for frequent access.

generated_parts.label: Service Port + Flex-Wiring Inspection Cover
generated_parts.spec: Flex_wiring closure module: service_loop inspection cover, strain_relief, bend_radius_note, connector keying, charge/debug service port, power vs signal separation label

requirements: Service loop access for cable maintenance and inspection
Thermal management via ambient air circulation
Power and signal separation for cable management
Strain relief at every flex boundary
Connector keying and labeling to prevent mis-mating
Visible bend radius notes for repeated flexion cycles
Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated)
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts
Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture

Subsystem mass <= 50g
Each part_tree child has corresponding geometry_ops
Adjacent interfaces named with matching joint families
Critical joints show insert/washer/locking/preload evidence

risk.mit: Add visible locking evidence such as inserts or washers to critical joints.

risk.mit: Ensure all connectors have clear keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of increased assembly time due to complex harness routing.
risk.mit: Simplify harness routing to reduce assembly complexity.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal issues if ambient air circulation is insufficient.
risk.mit: Ensure adequate thermal management through improved air circulation.

risk.desc: Risk of service access obstruction if cable routing is not properly managed.
risk.mit: Maintain clear service access paths for all cables and connectors.
